rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, November 23, 2010, line 1 PLAINTIFF SCOTT PEREIRA'S Notice Of Motion And Motion To Disqualify Counsel For Defts Guy Stilson, Esq., And Low Ball & Lynch OFF CALENDAR PER MOVING PARTY. =(302/CWW)

rulings.Case Title: PRINCESS NORMAN VS. KRISTINA ZEBRATSKI et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Defendant Crawley Warren Insurance Services, Inc'S Amended Notice Of Motion For Summary Judgment/Adjudication
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, November 23, 2010, line 10 Defendant Crawley Warren Insurance Services, Inc'S Amended Notice Of Motion For Summary Judgment/Adjudication. MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT IS GRANTED. PLAINTIFF DOES NOT PROVIDE A SEPARATE STATEMENT THAT RESPONDS TO EACH OF THE FACTS THE MOVING PARTY CONTENDS ARE UNDISPUTED AND IDENTFYING ANY OTHER MATERIAL FACTS PLAINTIFF CONTENDS ARE DISPUTED. CRC 3.1350(E); CCP 437C (B)(3). PLAINTIFFS' RESPONSE TO DEFENDANT'S SEPARATE STATEMENT CONCEDES ALL OF THE ESSENTIAL FACTS STATED BY DFENFANT. DFENDANT SHIFTS THE BURDEN OF PROOF AND PLAINTIFF FAILS TO SUBMIT EVIDENCE RAISING A TRABLE ISSUE OF FACT. DEFENDANT MUST PREPARE AN ORDER GRANTING THE MOTION THAT REFERENCES AND ATTACHES ITS SEPARATE STATEMENT. SEE CCP 437C(G). =(302/CWW)

rulings.Case Title: WILLIAM D. PALLAS et al VS. SAN FRANCISCO MUNICIPAL TRANSPORT AGENCY (SFMTA) et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Mtn On Petition Of Writ Of Mandate
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, November 23, 2010, line 15 PLAINTIFF WILLIAM PALLAS, GERSON GARCIA, DANIEL MCGUFFIN, ROBERT CONRAD, SALVADOR DEL REAL'S Notice Of Motion On Petition Of Writ Of Mandate IS DENIED. EXISTING LAW CONTROLS THE APPLICATION OF EQUITABLE RELIEF. DEFENDANT CAN PRESENTLY HOLD MEDALLIONS FOR SALE AND PLAINTIFFS DO NOT SUFFICIENTLY DISTINGUISH CITY OF WATSONVILLE V. STATE DEPARTMENT OF HEALTH SERVICES (2005) 133 CAL APP 4TH 875, PROFESSIONAL ENGINEERS IN CALIF. GOVT. V. SCHWARZENEGGER (2010) 50 CAL 4TH 989, AND MARINE FORESTS SOCIETY V. CALIFORNIA COASTAL COMM. (2005) 36 CAL 4TH 1. THE BOARD ULTIMATELY RATIFIED DEFENDANT'S CONDUCT. PLAINTIFFS FAIL TO SUFFCIENTLY DEMONSTRATE A MANDATORY DUTY. =(302/CWW)
